title: On Aging 2026 Kicks Off in Atlanta as Dubai Congress Opens Simultaneously
description: Thousands of aging professionals convened at the Hyatt Regency Atlanta for Day 1 of the American Society on Aging's On Aging 2026 conference, themed 'The Power of Belonging.' Meanwhile, the 11th Global Congress on Aging and Gerontology opened in Dubai with the theme 'The Future of Aging: Science, Sustainability, and Society,' creating an unprecedented multi-continent week of longevity discourse.

title: Homoharringtonine Identified as Potent Senotherapeutic in Nature Communications Publication
description: Published in Nature Communications, a screen of 2,150 FDA-approved drugs identified homoharringtonine (HHT), a leukemia therapeutic, as a senotherapeutic that clears senescent adipocytes, restores metabolic function, and extends lifespan in both progeroid and naturally aged mice. HHT's existing approval could dramatically shorten the path to human senolytic trials.

title: Mitrix Bio Moves Mitochondrial Transplants Into the Clinic for First Human Trials
description: Mitrix Bio is advancing mitochondrial transplantation into human clinical trials, a novel approach to treating age-related mitochondrial decline. The Longevity.Technology coverage marks a major step for a therapy modality that could address fundamental drivers of aging including energy metabolism collapse and cellular senescence.

title: Barcelona Supercomputing Center Study: Female Immune Systems Age Much Faster Than Male
description: A new study from the Barcelona Supercomputing Center reveals that the female immune system changes much more than the male system with age. This finding has major implications for sex-specific aging interventions and explains divergent outcomes observed in trials like PEARL that showed female-specific benefits from rapamycin.

title: A4M Longevity SpringFest 2026 Forecasts: Biological Age Testing Enters Routine Care, Brainspan Gets Attention
description: More than 3,000 practitioners at A4M's Longevity SpringFest identified five emerging care trends: biological age testing becoming a routine baseline marker, brainspan emerging as the new longevity endpoint, stress becoming a primary therapeutic target, personalized protocols, and informed self-directed patients becoming the norm. GrimAge, DunedinPACE, and OmicAge are leading adoption.

title: Homoharringtonine (HHT) Identified as Senotherapeutic That Extends Lifespan in Mice
description: Screen of 2,150 approved drugs identified HHT as a senotherapeutic that clears senescent adipocytes, restores white adipose tissue function, lowers inflammation, and extends lifespan in progeroid and naturally aged mice.

title: Mitrix Bio Moves Mitochondria Transplants Into Clinical Trials
description: Mitrix Bio advances mitochondrial transplantation into human trials, targeting age-related mitochondrial dysfunction as a foundational aging mechanism.

title: Terbinafine and Miglustat: FDA-Approved Drugs Extend C. elegans Lifespan via UPRmt
description: Two FDA-approved drugs -- terbinafine (antifungal) and miglustat (Gaucher disease) -- extend worm lifespan by activating the mitochondrial unfolded protein response, with existing safety profiles potentially accelerating human trials.

title: BSC Study Reveals Female Immune System Changes Much More Than Men With Age
description: First-of-its-kind analysis from BSC shows the female immune system undergoes substantially greater age-related changes than the male system, with implications for sex-specific aging therapies.

title: On Aging 2026 Conference Day 1 Opens at Hyatt Regency Atlanta
description: The nation's largest multidisciplinary aging conference kicked off with pre-conference workshops on April 20. Theme 'The Power of Belonging' runs through April 23, covering policy, research, clinical practice, and aging services.

title: 11th Global Congress on Aging and Gerontology Opens in Dubai
description: Two-day hybrid event (April 20-21) in Dubai focuses on 'The Future of Aging: Science, Sustainability, and Society' with sessions on cellular repair and longevity technology.

title: A4M Longevity SpringFest 2026: 3,000+ Practitioners Shape Future-Ready Longevity Medicine
description: Record attendance at A4M's spring gathering in West Palm Beach featured 90+ sessions on biological age testing, brainspan, stress as a therapeutic target, and integrated longevity protocols.

title: Box A of HMGB1 Plasmid Reverses Age-Related Plasma Proteomic Changes in Perimenopausal Monkeys
description: Box A of HMGB1 plasmid treatment reversed age-related changes in the plasma proteomic profile of perimenopausal monkeys, suggesting potential for targeting menopause-related aging acceleration.

title: PEPITEM Replacement Therapy Shows Potential for Early-Stage Inflammatory Arthritis
description: Birmingham researchers demonstrate PEPITEM replacement therapy could modulate inflammation in early-stage inflammatory arthritis, offering a path for age-related inflammatory conditions.

title: ITP Reports 11 Compounds Including Astaxanthin and Alpha-Ketoglutarate Fail to Extend Mouse Lifespan
description: NIA Interventions Testing Program tested 11 compounds in UM-HET3 mice across three sites. None extended lifespan, with some reducing female lifespan -- underscoring translation difficulty.

title: Engineered Stem Cells Become Lifelong Protein Factories Against HIV, Malaria, and Flu
description: Rockefeller researchers engineered hematopoietic stem cells to produce antibodies indefinitely. As few as 29 edited cells produced measurable levels, with potential applications for any therapeutic protein.

title: Day 1 of 2026 Optimal Aging Conference at University of Louisville Draws 200+ Attendees
description: The Optimal Aging Conference convened at University of Louisville Shelby Campus with sessions on evidence-based longevity practices and community aging.

title: Longevity Segment Investability Tracker: Cellular Reprogramming Leads With $1.28B Raised
description: Two-year tracker shows cellular reprogramming at $1.28B (6 deals), age-related disease drugs at $835M (11 deals). Insilico Medicine alone has accumulated $3.6B in pharma deal value.

title: USPTO Publishes AI-Driven Senolytic Compound Patent Covering 800,000+ Molecules
description: Patent application US20260097022A1 discloses screening of 2,352 compounds for senolytic activity and neural networks trained to predict activity across 800,000+ molecules targeting Bcl-2 proteins.

title: Life Biosciences' ER-100 Trial: Is the FDA Warming to Rejuvenation Therapies?
description: FDA cleared Life Biosciences' IND filing for ER-100, an epigenetic treatment for partial cellular reprogramming in the human eye, signaling a possible regulatory route for longevity interventions.

title: GPR133 Receptor Activated by AP503 Boosts Bone Density in Mice, Counters Osteoporosis
description: Leipzig scientists found that activating GPR133 receptor with compound AP503 significantly increased bone strength in mice and counteracted osteoporosis, with potential implications for aging populations.

title: Longevity Market Funding: Blueprint $60M, Function Health $298M, Aeovian $55M
description: Recent longevity funding includes Blueprint's $60M (backed by Kim Kardashian, Logan Paul), Function Health's $298M Series B, and Aeovian's $55M for mTORC1 inhibitors.

title: Longevity Biotech Reaches $3.74B in Q1 2026, Up 56% Year-Over-Year Across 49 Deals
description: Q1 2026 financing data shows $3.74B across 49 events, a 56% YoY increase. Full-year projections converge on $8-9B for 2026, implying 55-60% growth.

title: Insilico Medicine and Eli Lilly Announce $2.75B AI-Driven R&D Collaboration
description: Lilly licensed assets from Insilico's AI-driven pipeline in a collaboration potentially worth up to $2.75B, signaling Big Pharma confidence in AI-driven longevity drug discovery.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches NIA-Funded Multi-Phase Rapamycin Clinical Trial
description: Randomized, placebo-controlled trial of approximately 84 older adults will evaluate rapamycin dosing, safety, and both short- and longer-term effects on healthy aging.

title: Laromestrocel Stem Cell Therapy Reverses Aging-Related Frailty in Phase 2b Trial
description: Phase 2b trial in 148 adults aged 70-85 showed a single dose of laromestrocel improved 6-minute walk test distance by 63.4 meters. About 30.8% of treated patients improved to non-frail status.

title: Daily Multivitamin Slows Biological Aging by Four Months in Harvard COSMOS Trial
description: COSMOS randomized clinical trial found daily multivitamin slowed five measures of epigenetic aging by approximately four months over two years, with greater benefits for biologically older participants.

title: ARPA-H Commits $144M Through PROSPR Program to Fund First Large-Scale Aging Trials
description: ARPA-H awarded $144M to seven teams to build groundwork for Phase 3 trials of healthspan interventions including rapamycin, SGLT-2 inhibitors, and semaglutide in healthy older adults.

title: Buck Institute THRIVE Secures $34.5M from ARPA-H to Build First FDA-Grade Aging Score
description: Stanford-led coalition with Buck Institute, Methuselah Foundation, and YMCA secured up to $34.5M from ARPA-H to develop FDA-grade Intrinsic Capacity Score through 1,000+ person trials.

title: Brown University and Rochester Launch $22M Trial of HIV Drug Censavudine to Slow Aging
description: Multi-institution ARPA-H project enrolls 200 adults aged 60-65 to test whether Censavudine can reduce DNA-triggered inflammation and slow aging. Clinical trial runs 48 weeks across three sites.
